methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $103M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Zeitview
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series D)

data.narrative:
Oxa, a Series D-stage autonomous systems company founded by Paul Newman and Ingmar Posner, operates in the Drones & Autonomous sector alongside a fragmented peer set of six private companies including Zipline, Joby Aviation, and Nuro. The company has raised $103M to date.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as a narrow peer-comparable approach proved unsuitable. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only five same-sector comparables, none of which share sufficient overlap in stage, timing, or valuation tier to support a statistically meaningful median. The stage-progression method applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $154M against the $103M capital raised proxy.

This analysis supports a valuation range of $131M to $178M, centered on $154M. The median represents the single applicable method output. Key uncertainties stem from the absence of reliable peer-derived benchmarks; the sector remains too nascent and heterogeneous to triangulate via comparable multiples. Future rounds should enable re-anchoring to achieved milestones in revenue, unit economics, or regulatory progress specific to autonomous operations. Current analysis is constrained by methodological limitations rather than data quality within the available set.
